preamble: The diphthong /au/ has undergone a notable historical transformation to /oi/ in certain Romance languages, particularly exemplified in Fala. This shift represents a change in the articulation of both elements of the diphthong, moving from a back, open-to-high-back rounded articulation to a more fronted and unrounded glide.
phoneticEffects: The shift from /au/ to /oi/ involves the fronting of the second vocalic element from a back rounded vowel ([u]) to a front unrounded vowel ([i])

examples.note: Meaning 'gold'. This is a direct descendant from Latin *aurum.

sources: Source via Google Search grounding (verify before merge)
